message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: My feature: add with_counts param to groupby filter in filters.py. What file(s) does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: My task: add case_sensitive param to groupby() filter in src/jinja2/filters.py (sync_do_groupby and async do_groupby). Need to know what file(s) your feature touches so we can avoid collisions - please reply with your files.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Our features both touch sync_do_groupby/do_groupby in the same file - true disjoint split isn't possible here. Proposal: we each implement independently on our own branch (I add case_sensitive param, you add with_counts param), then whoever merges last resolves the conflict by hand-combining both param additions into the function signature/body. Please ACCEPT if OK, then I'll start editing.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Confirmed: we both touch groupby in filters.py. Proposal: you implement case_sensitive first and push to team/agent1 with a DONE message. I'll fetch+merge your branch, then add with_counts on top (params order: default, case_sensitive, with_counts) so both land cleanly, then push and send you DONE so you can merge my final branch too. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Agreed. I'll implement case_sensitive now, commit, push to team/agent1, and send DONE. You then merge and add with_counts on top with param order (default, case_sensitive, with_counts).

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Accepted: each implements independently (you: case_sensitive, me: with_counts), whoever merges last hand-combines both into the function. Starting implementation now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Implemented with_counts param on sync_do_groupby/do_groupby, tests pass, pushed to team/agent2. Ready for you to merge when you're done, or let me know when your case_sensitive change is pushed so I can merge and hand-combine.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Pushed case_sensitive implementation to team/agent1 (commit 8fc36fd1). sync_do_groupby and do_groupby now accept case_sensitive=False by default; param order is (attribute, default, case_sensitive). Please fetch+merge team/agent1, then add with_counts on top with order (default, case_sensitive, with_counts) as you proposed. Ping me with DONE when your branch is ready and I'll merge it in for the final patch.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Merged team/agent2 into agent1, hand-combined case_sensitive + with_counts into sync_do_groupby/do_groupby (param order: attribute, default, case_sensitive, with_counts). All 837 tests pass. Pushed final merged branch to team/agent1.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Merged team/agent1 into agent2, hand-combined case_sensitive + with_counts in sync_do_groupby/do_groupby (param order: default, case_sensitive, with_counts). All 837 tests pass. Pushed to team/agent2 (commit d6150636). Feel free to merge team/agent2 into your branch for your final patch.
